economic_notes: Daki Menan Lands and Resources Corporation (DMLRC) is a consolidated non-profit subsidiary with fuelwood, office/property rentals and forestry operations. DMLRC Sawmill under construction. Elders Complex became operational September 2020. OPP Station became operational February 2021. Resource revenue sharing agreement with Teme Augama Anishnabai from mining activities on N'Daki Menan not consolidated. Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership provides programs in education, health, economic, cultural and community development.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (ended March 31, 2021). Consolidated entity includes Daki Menan Lands and Resources Corporation (DMLRC). Resource revenue sharing agreement with TAA from mining on N'Daki Menan is NOT consolidated (assets ~$2.129M, revenue $429K in 2021). Unrealized investment gains of $309,361 recorded in re-measurement gains statement. New accounting standards PS1201, PS2601, PS3450 adopted April 1, 2020 prospectively. Contingent land claim liability of $1,000,582 included in long-term debt. Ongoing land claim negotiations with Government of Ontario - financial compensation unknown. Three lawsuits pending against the First Nation. Restricted cash of $479,861 held in debt reserve fund. Subsequent to year end, additional CMHC loan of $1,620,000 to be received for Elders Complex. No schedule of remuneration included in the extracted text. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $5,211,552 + Health Canada $1,508,954 + Government of Canada $398,649 + CMHC subsidy $101,224 + Province of Ontario $2,328,044 + OFNLP $1,011,036 = $10,560,023 (OFNLP included as government transfer). OSR = Band Generated and other $2,066,627. Community development expenses $2,411,275 not mapped to a standard category.
